events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The legendary Rolling Stones frontman, Mick Jagger, celebrates his 83rd birthday.

angles.name: Rock Icon Still Rolling
angles.copy: Eighty-three years young and still commanding the stage! Wishing a very happy birthday to the one and only Mick Jagger.

events.title: MTV launched in 1981
events.blurb: Music Television, MTV, launched on this day in 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' famously being the first video ever played.

angles.name: The Day Video Killed... (Or Did It?)
angles.copy: Forty-five years ago, MTV burst onto screens, forever changing music and pop culture. The first video? 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. The irony is not lost on us.

events.title: Oasis released 'Definitely Maybe' in 1994
events.blurb: Oasis released their iconic debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on this day in 1994, propelling them to superstardom.

angles.name: Britpop's Big Bang
angles.copy: It's been 32 years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and changed the sound of a generation. What's your favourite track from the album?

events.title: Woodstock Festival began in 1969
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Festival of Music & Art began on this day in 1969, becoming a pivotal moment in music history.

angles.name: Peace, Love & Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago today, half a million people gathered for three days of peace, love, and music at Woodstock. An unforgettable cultural moment.

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: Today marks the anniversary of the death of Elvis Presley, who would have been 91 this year.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: On this day, we remember the one and only Elvis Presley. His music and legend continue to live on, 49 years after his passing.

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's death
events.blurb: Today marks 30 years since the tragic death of rap icon Tupac Shakur, who would have been 55.

angles.name: Tupac's Enduring Legacy
angles.copy: Three decades ago, we lost a true music pioneer. Tupac Shakur's influence on hip-hop and culture remains as strong as ever.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death
events.blurb: Today marks the anniversary of the death of guitar legend Jimi Hendrix, who would have been 84.

angles.name: Remembering The Guitar God
angles.copy: Fifty-six years ago, the world lost a true musical visionary. Jimi Hendrix's revolutionary guitar playing continues to inspire.

events.title: The Beatles released 'Abbey Road' in 1969
events.blurb: The Beatles' iconic 'Abbey Road' album, featuring that legendary zebra crossing cover, was released on this day in 1969.

angles.name: That Iconic Crossing
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. The music is timeless, and that album cover? Still one of the most famous ever.

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The Pretenders frontwoman Chrissie Hynde celebrates her 75th birthday today.

angles.name: Happy Birthday, Chrissie Hynde
angles.copy: A rock icon celebrating 75 years! Happy birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde, whose voice and attitude continue to captivate.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Oasis and Beady Eye frontman Liam Gallagher celebrates his 54th birthday.

angles.name: Still Mad For It
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! The rock 'n' roll star still knows how to make headlines and brilliant music.

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' single released in 1975
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic single 'Imagine' was released on this day in the UK in 1975, though the album came out in 1971.

angles.name: A Song For Peace
angles.copy: Fifty-one years since the album, and 49 since the UK single release, John Lennon's 'Imagine' continues to resonate globally. A timeless message for our times.

events.title: Apple launched the first iPod in 2001
events.blurb: On this day in 2001, Apple introduced the very first iPod, revolutionising how we listen to music.

angles.name: 25 Years of the iPod
angles.copy: Remember your first iPod? Twenty-five years ago, this little device changed everything about carrying your music collection.

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated worldwide as James Bond Day, marking the anniversary of the first 007 film, Dr. No, in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Raise a martini (shaken, not stirred) to 64 years of iconic spy cinema. What's your favourite Bond film or theme song?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: Today marks the birthday of musical legend John Lennon, who would have been 86.

angles.name: Remembering A Legend
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the enduring legacy of John Lennon – a true musical icon and visionary.

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: The comedic genius and Monty Python star John Cleese celebrates his 87th birthday.

angles.name: And Now For Something Completely Different
angles.copy: Happy 87th birthday to the brilliant John Cleese! From Monty Python to Fawlty Towers, his comedic legacy is truly legendary.

events.title: The Beatles released 'The White Album' in 1968
events.blurb: The Beatles' self-titled double album, famously known as 'The White Album', was released on this day in 1968.

angles.name: Fifty-Eight Years of The White Album
angles.copy: The Beatles' ambitious 'White Album' dropped 58 years ago today. A sprawling, eclectic masterpiece that still sparks debate among fans.
